chapters.title: First Contact with the Runtime
chapters.storyPurpose: Alex arrives at the station and witnesses the cascading system failures firsthand, meeting Commander Torres who explains the Bun migration plan
chapters.learningPurpose: Install Bun and understand what it is as a JavaScript runtime alternative to Node.js
chapters.docQuery: bun installation getting started what is bun
chapters.narrativeSetup: Alex steps off the transport shuttle into the Engineering Command Center as alarms blare and holographic displays flash red with system warnings. Commander Torres greets them with urgent news: the station's JavaScript runtime is failing under the load of atmospheric data processing.
chapters.narrativePayoff: With Bun successfully installed on a test terminal, Alex sees their first glimpse of hope as the runtime initializes faster than expected. Torres nods approvingly: 'Now we can begin the real work.'

chapters.title: Running the Diagnostics
chapters.storyPurpose: Alex learns to execute JavaScript files with Bun while helping Dr. Vasquez run critical atmospheric analysis scripts
chapters.learningPurpose: Execute JavaScript files using Bun and understand basic runtime commands
chapters.docQuery: bun run javascript files commands cli
chapters.narrativeSetup: Dr. Vasquez urgently needs her atmospheric analysis scripts running again, but the current Node.js runtime keeps timing out on the massive datasets. Alex must prove Bun can handle the computational load.
chapters.narrativePayoff: The atmospheric analysis completes in record time, and Dr. Vasquez's frustrated expression transforms into amazement. 'These processing speeds... we might actually make our deadline,' she says, looking at Alex with newfound respect.

chapters.title: Managing the Dependencies
chapters.storyPurpose: The station's package management system is corrupted, and Alex must use Bun's built-in package manager to restore critical system dependencies
chapters.learningPurpose: Use Bun as a package manager to install, update, and manage JavaScript dependencies
chapters.docQuery: bun install package manager dependencies npm packages
chapters.narrativeSetup: ARIA reports that the station's npm cache is corrupted and several critical system modules are missing dependencies. The backup systems won't engage without these packages, leaving the station vulnerable.
chapters.narrativePayoff: As the last dependency installs at lightning speed, backup systems throughout the station hum back to life. Marcus calls from Communications: 'Whatever you just did, the transmission buffers are stable again.'

chapters.title: Testing Under Pressure
chapters.storyPurpose: A critical system update needs validation before deployment, but the testing infrastructure is down - Alex must use Bun's test runner while the station faces a power fluctuation crisis
chapters.learningPurpose: Write and run tests using Bun's built-in test runner
chapters.docQuery: bun test testing framework test runner
chapters.narrativeSetup: Power fluctuations from the planet's electromagnetic storms threaten to destabilize the newly migrated systems. Torres insists that no code goes live without proper testing, but the external testing framework is offline.
chapters.narrativePayoff: The tests pass just as another power surge hits the station, but this time the systems hold steady. Torres grips Alex's shoulder: 'Good work. But the real test is coming - we need to bundle everything for the final migration.'

chapters.title: Bundling for Survival
chapters.storyPurpose: The station's modular systems need to be consolidated into optimized bundles for the final migration, while dealing with increasing system instability
chapters.learningPurpose: Use Bun's bundler to optimize and package JavaScript applications for production
chapters.docQuery: bun build bundler optimization production bundle
chapters.narrativeSetup: The Cascade is accelerating - system failures are spreading faster than they can be patched. Alex must bundle all the station's critical systems into optimized packages that can be deployed simultaneously before total system collapse.
chapters.narrativePayoff: The bundled systems are ready for deployment, compressed and optimized beyond what the old infrastructure could achieve. But as Alex prepares for the final migration, ARIA's voice carries an ominous warning: 'Multiple cascade failures detected. Recommend immediate system-wide deployment.'

chapters.title: The Final Migration
chapters.storyPurpose: Alex leads the complete migration of all station systems to Bun during a critical atmospheric data transmission window to Earth
chapters.learningPurpose: Deploy a complete Bun-based application architecture combining runtime, package management, testing, and bundling
chapters.docQuery: bun production deployment full stack application
chapters.narrativeSetup: Earth's transmission window opens in minutes, and Dr. Vasquez's complete atmospheric analysis must reach the colony selection committee. Alex must orchestrate the final migration of all systems to Bun while the old infrastructure crumbles around them.
chapters.narrativePayoff: The migration completes seconds before total system failure would have occurred. All station systems now run on Bun's lightning-fast runtime, the atmospheric data streams flawlessly to Earth, and Alex has proven themselves as the engineer who saved humanity's next colony world.

narrativeThread: Alex must master each aspect of Bun - runtime, package management, testing, and bundling - to systematically replace the station's failing JavaScript infrastructure before cascading system failures prevent the transmission of critical atmospheric data to Earth. Each successful migration brings the station closer to stability while Alex grows from an uncertain newcomer into the engineer who saves the mission.
resolutionPlan: The final chapter requires Alex to combine all learned Bun capabilities - using the runtime to execute the atmospheric analysis, the package manager to ensure all dependencies are current, the test runner to validate system integrity, and the bundler to optimize everything for the critical Earth transmission. This demonstrates complete mastery while resolving the central question of whether Alex can restore the station's systems and deliver the data that determines humanity's future.
